# MSBuild Project Review — ContosoApp Solution

## Solution Overview

| Project | SDK | TFM | Role |
|---|---|---|---|
| `src/ContosoApp.Core` | Microsoft.NET.Sdk | net8.0 | Core library |
| `src/ContosoApp.Services` | Microsoft.NET.Sdk | net8.0 | Services (→ Core) |
| `src/ContosoApp.Api` | Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web | net8.0 | Web API (→ Core, Services) |
| `test/ContosoApp.Core.Tests` | Microsoft.NET.Sdk | net8.0 | Unit tests for Core |
| `test/ContosoApp.Api.Tests` | Microsoft.NET.Sdk | net8.0 | Unit tests for API |

**No `Directory.Build.props`, `Directory.Build.targets`, or `Directory.Packages.props` exist.**

---

## 🔴 Errors (Likely to Cause Issues)

### E1: NuGet Package Version Skew Across Projects

Multiple projects reference the **same package at different versions**, which can cause runtime `FileNotFoundException`, `MissingMethodException`, or subtle behavioral differences.

| Package | Core | Services | Api | Core.Tests | Api.Tests |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions` | **8.0.0** | **8.0.1** | **8.0.0** | — | — |
| `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | — | — | — | **17.10.0** | **17.9.0** |
| `xunit` | — | — | — | **2.8.1** | **2.7.1** |
| `xunit.runner.visualstudio` | — | — | — | **2.8.1** | **2.5.6** |

**Impact**: When `ContosoApp.Api` loads both `Core` (logging 8.0.0) and `Services` (logging 8.0.1), NuGet's unification picks the higher version — but this happens silently and can mask binding issues. The test SDK/xunit skew means the two test projects may behave differently or use different test discovery behavior.

**Fix**: Adopt Central Package Management (see Suggestion S1 below) to enforce a single version per package across the entire solution.

---

## 🟡 Warnings (Anti-Patterns to Fix)

### W1: Massive Property Duplication Across All 5 Projects

Every single project file repeats this identical 7-property block:

```xml
<TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
<Nullable>enable</Nullable>
<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
<TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
<Company>Contoso</Company>
<Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
```

**Impact**: When you need to change TFM, toggle a setting, or update company info, you must edit 5 files. Easy to miss one (as demonstrated by the version skew above — copy-paste drift is inevitable).

**Fix**: Create a `Directory.Build.props` at the repo root (see S2 below).

### W2: DocumentationFile Setting Duplicated in 3 Source Projects

All three `src/` projects repeat:
```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
</PropertyGroup>
```

Also note the **split PropertyGroup** anti-pattern: this is in a second `<PropertyGroup>` block separated from the main one, with no condition to justify the split. This reduces readability.

**Fix**: Move `DocumentationFile` into `Directory.Build.props` (conditionally, for `src/` projects only) or into a `src/Directory.Build.props`.

### W3: Custom Target `PrintBuildInfo` Copy-Pasted Across 3 Source Projects

All three `src/` projects have an identical target:
```xml
<Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
  <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
</Target>
```

**Impact**: Duplication. If you change the message format, you need to update 3 files.

**Fix**: Move to `Directory.Build.targets` at the repo root (or a `src/Directory.Build.targets`).

### W4: Custom Target Missing `Inputs`/`Outputs`

The `PrintBuildInfo` target has no `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes. This means MSBuild will **always** execute it, even on no-op builds. While it's just a `Message` task (so the performance impact is negligible), it's a bad precedent — if someone later adds file operations to this target, it will break incremental build.

**Fix**: Since this is a diagnostic message that _should_ run every build, this is acceptable as-is. But consider annotating it clearly, or removing it for CI builds:
```xml
<Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build"
        Condition="'$(CI)' != 'true'">
```

### W5: Test Project Properties Duplicated

Both test projects repeat `<IsPackable>false</IsPackable>` and the same test package triplet (`Test.Sdk` + `xunit` + `xunit.runner.visualstudio` + `NSubstitute`). This should be consolidated.

---

## 🔵 Suggestions (Improvements)

### S1: Adopt Central Package Management (CPM)

Create a `Directory.Packages.props` at the repo root to centralize all NuGet versions. This **eliminates version skew entirely** (addresses E1).

**Proposed `Directory.Packages.props`:**

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <!-- Shared -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />

    <!-- Web -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" Version="6.5.0" />

    <!-- Testing -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.10.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="NSubstitute" Version="5.1.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

Then each `.csproj` drops the `Version` attribute:
```xml
<!-- Before -->
<PackageReference Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />

<!-- After -->
<PackageReference Include="xunit" />
```

### S2: Create `Directory.Build.props` for Shared Properties

**Proposed `Directory.Build.props` at repo root:**

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**Proposed `src/Directory.Build.props`** (for source projects only):

```xml
<Project>
  <!-- Import parent Directory.Build.props -->
  <Import Project="$([MSBuild]::GetPathOfFileAbove('Directory.Build.props', '$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)../'))" />

  <PropertyGroup>
    <GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

> **Note**: Use `<GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>` instead of the explicit `<DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>`. The SDK already computes the path automatically when this is `true` — the explicit path is restating an SDK default.

**Proposed `test/Directory.Build.props`** (for test projects only):

```xml
<Project>
  <Import Project="$([MSBuild]::GetPathOfFileAbove('Directory.Build.props', '$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)../'))" />

  <PropertyGroup>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" />
    <PackageReference Include="NSubstitute" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

### S3: Create `Directory.Build.targets` for Shared Targets

**Proposed `src/Directory.Build.targets`:**

```xml
<Project>
  <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
    <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
  </Target>
</Project>
```

### S4: Resulting Minimal Project Files

After S1 + S2 + S3, the project files become dramatically simpler:

**`src/ContosoApp.Core/ContosoApp.Core.csproj`** (from 26 → 8 lines):
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />
    <PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

**`src/ContosoApp.Services/ContosoApp.Services.csproj`** (from 30 → 11 lines):
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />
    <PackageReference Include="Newtonsoft.Json" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\ContosoApp.Core\ContosoApp.Core.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

**`src/ContosoApp.Api/ContosoApp.Api.csproj`** (from 32 → 13 lines):
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web">
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />
    <PackageReference Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" />
    <PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\ContosoApp.Core\ContosoApp.Core.csproj" />
    <ProjectReference Include="..\ContosoApp.Services\ContosoApp.Services.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

**`test/ContosoApp.Core.Tests/ContosoApp.Core.Tests.csproj`** (from 25 → 7 lines):
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\..\src\ContosoApp.Core\ContosoApp.Core.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

**`test/ContosoApp.Api.Tests/ContosoApp.Api.Tests.csproj`** (from 25 → 7 lines):
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\..\src\ContosoApp.Api\ContosoApp.Api.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

**Total: from ~138 lines of XML across 5 files → ~46 lines + 4 centralized config files.** Each project now contains only what makes it _unique_.

### S5: Explicit `DocumentationFile` Path Restates SDK Default

In all three source projects:
```xml
<DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
```

This is the **exact path the SDK computes automatically** when `<GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>` is set. The explicit path is unnecessary boilerplate. Replace with just `<GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>` (already covered in S2).

### S6: Consider Adding `PrivateAssets` to Development-Only Packages

`xunit.runner.visualstudio` is a test infrastructure adapter that should not flow transitively. While test projects are rarely referenced by other projects, it's good hygiene:

```xml
<PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" PrivateAssets="all" />
```

This would go in the `test/Directory.Build.props` from S2.

---

## 🟢 Positive (Things Done Well)

- ✅ **All projects are modern SDK-style** — no legacy `<Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)..."` patterns
- ✅ **No `packages.config`** — all using `PackageReference` 
- ✅ **No manual `AssemblyInfo.cs`** — using auto-generated assembly attributes
- ✅ **No explicit `<Compile Include="...">`** — correctly relying on SDK implicit globbing
- ✅ **`TreatWarningsAsErrors` is enabled everywhere** — excellent for code quality
- ✅ **`EnforceCodeStyleInBuild` is enabled** — ensures consistent style in CI
- ✅ **`Nullable` enabled** — using modern nullability analysis
- ✅ **Clean dependency graph** — Core has no project refs, Services→Core, Api→Core+Services; no cycles
- ✅ **Test projects marked `IsPackable=false`** — correct practice

---

## Summary — Priority Action Items

| # | Severity | Issue | Effort |
|---|---|---|---|
| E1 | 🔴 | Package version skew (logging 8.0.0 vs 8.0.1, xunit 2.8.1 vs 2.7.1, etc.) | Medium |
| W1 | 🟡 | 7 properties copy-pasted across 5 files | Low |
| W2 | 🟡 | `DocumentationFile` duplicated + uses explicit path instead of `GenerateDocumentationFile` | Low |
| W3 | 🟡 | `PrintBuildInfo` target copy-pasted across 3 files | Low |
| W5 | 🟡 | Test infrastructure duplicated in both test projects | Low |
| S1 | 🔵 | Adopt Central Package Management | Medium |
| S2 | 🔵 | Create `Directory.Build.props` hierarchy (root + src/ + test/) | Low |
| S3 | 🔵 | Create `src/Directory.Build.targets` for shared targets | Low |

**Recommended order**: S2 (Directory.Build.props) → S1 (CPM) → S3 (Directory.Build.targets) → verify with `dotnet build` + `dotnet test`.
